verses.9: Not only was the Teacher wise, but he also taught the people knowledge; he pondered, searched out, and arranged many proverbs.
verses.10: The Teacher searched to find delightful sayings and to record accurate words of truth.
verses.11: The words of the wise are like goads, and the anthologies of the masters are like firmly embedded nails driven by a single Shepherd.
verses.12: And by these, my son, be further warned: There is no end to the making of many books, and much study wearies the body.
verses.13: When all has been heard, the conclusion of the matter is this: Fear God and keep His commandments, because this is the whole duty of man.
verses.14: For God will bring every deed into judgment, along with every hidden thing, whether good or evil.
